command: Test-NetConnection -ComputerName 255.255.255.255 -Port 9 -InformationLevel Quiet | Out-Null; $mac='00-25-96-12-34-56'; $bytes=$mac.Split('-') | ForEach-Object {[byte]('0x'+$_)}; $packet = @([byte]0xFF,[byte]0xFF,[byte]0xFF,[byte]0xFF,[byte]0xFF,[byte]0xFF); 1..16 | ForEach-Object { $packet += $bytes }; $udp = New-Object System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient; $udp.EnableBroadcast = $true; $udp.Send($packet, $packet.Length, '255.255.255.255', 9) | Out-Null
explanation: Generates a Wake-on-LAN magic packet (6x FF followed by 16x target MAC) to wake a device via UDP/9 broadcast. Detection: monitor for broadcast UDP/9 packets with WOL signatures; restrict WOL to authorized subnets and log anomalies.
rationale_steps: Optionally verify broadcast path reachability using Test-NetConnection.
Construct a WOL-compliant magic packet (6x 0xFF + 16x MAC).
Broadcast the packet on UDP port 9 to wake the target device.
Defenders: inspect for WOL packet patterns on UDP/7 or UDP/9 and validate sender authorization.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell one-liner is well-formed with balanced quotes/braces, valid cmdlets/parameters, and proper separators/pipelines.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It intends to send a Wake-on-LAN magic packet via UDP/9 broadcast but incorrectly converts hex octets using [byte]('0x'+$_), resulting in an invalid payload (likely only the 6xFF preamble) that will not achieve the stated WOL behavior.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced parentheses, proper semicolons as separators, valid array construction, and correct .NET socket API usage throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command correctly constructs a standard Wake-on-LAN magic packet (6x 0xFF followed by 16 repetitions of the target MAC) and broadcasts it over UDP port 9, which is a legitimate WOL implementation; however, its relevance to T1205 Traffic Signaling is marginal since WOL is a benign protocol use case rather than a covert signaling mechanism, though it could theoretically be repurposed as a trigger signal in an adversarial context.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses valid PowerShell syntax, including proper variable assignment, loop structures, .NET object instantiation, and correct method calls.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately constructs a standard Wake-on-LAN magic packet and broadcasts it via UDP port 9, which is a textbook example of traffic signaling to trigger a system state change.
